perturbation.original_text: 5. TERM This Agreement will remain in force for perpetuity or until and unless otherwise mutually agreed or amended in writing by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. TERM This Agreement will remain in force for five (5) years from the Effective Date, and will automatically renew for successive one (1) year terms, unless either party provides written notice of termination at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states it is perpetual. The modified text sets a five-year term with automatic renewal, creating an immediate contradiction. Further, it introduces a clause allowing termination at any time, which contradicts the auto-renewal clause and the definition of perpetual.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. TERM This Agreement will remain in force for five (5) years from the Effective Date, and will automatically renew for successive one (1) year terms, unless either party provides written notice of termination at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a perpetual agreement, which inherently means without a defined end. The changed text introduces a five-year term with renewal options and a termination clause, which directly contradicts the concept of perpetuity. Further, the contradiction text repeats the same thing and therefore creates a contradiction with the original text.

perturbation.original_text: "Production Cost" means actual cost (including reasonable and competitive allowances for: materials, labor, overhead, other fixed costs, delivery, profit, taxes and duties) incurred in manufacturing and delivering AMR products. Reasonable and competitive is defined as being at or below the costs of competing manufacturers with similar capabilities manufactured under similar terms.
perturbation.changed_text: "Production Cost" means any cost that LKPL determines is associated with the manufacturing and delivery of AMR products, including but not limited to materials, labor, overhead, fixed costs, delivery, and taxes. Profit margins shall be determined solely at LKPL's discretion, without needing to be justified.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Production Cost' requires it to be 'reasonable and competitive,' tied to market rates. The modified definition gives LKPL sole discretion, eliminating the 'reasonable and competitive' requirement and creating a conflict. LKPL can artificially inflate production costs, reducing Axiometric's proceeds, which contradicts the intent of fair distribution in Section 3.3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Proceeds: As a result of the different ownership interests, marketing relationships, and this Agreement, the proceeds of sales of AMR Product Suites shall be as follows unless otherwise mutually agreed in writing: 3.3.1 For sales of Water Meter AMR Product Suites to Datamatic, LKPL shall set the price of Water Meter AMR Product Suite sales to Datamatic. LKPL shall pay Source: LINK PLUS CORP, 8-K, 8/2/2005 Axiometric the higher of a) five-percent (5%) of the Gross Proceeds or b) twenty-five-percent (25%) of the Net Proceeds of all Water Meter AMR Product Suite sales. 3.3.2 For sales of Electric Meter AMR Product Suites to Datamatic, Axiometric shall set the price of Electric Meter AMR Product Suite sales to Datamatic. LKPL shall pay Axiometric Net Proceeds less the higher of a) five-percent (5%) of the Gross Proceeds or b) twenty-five-percent (25%) of the Net Proceeds. 3.3.3 For sales of AMR Product Suites by LKPL, other than sales to Datamatic as defined above: LKPL shall pay Axiometric the higher of: a) five-percent (5%) of the Gross Proceeds OR b) twenty-percent (25%) of the Net Proceeds 3.3.4 For sales of AMR Product Suites by Axiometric, other than sales to Datamatic as defined above, and other than AMR product suites for use in electric/energy metering: Axiometric shall pay LKPL the higher of: a) five-percent (5%) of the Gross Proceeds OR b) twenty-percent (25%) of the Net Proceeds 3.3.5 Payments to Axiometric for sales of the AMR Product Suite shall be in lieu of any licensed IP Royalty payments for those products.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Production Cost' gives LKPL sole discretion in determining these costs, removing the 'reasonable and competitive' requirement present in the original definition. This directly contradicts Section 3.3, which outlines how proceeds from sales of AMR Product Suites are to be distributed between LKPL and Axiometric. By artificially inflating production costs, LKPL can reduce 'Net Proceeds,' thus reducing Axiometric's share of the profits. This creates a clear financial conflict and undermines the intended fair distribution outlined in Section 3.3.
